caption:
JavaScript switch is embarrassing next to Rust match.

Pattern matching, destructuring, exhaustive checks — all in one expression. No break statements. No fallthrough bugs.

Which pattern would you ship? A or B?

#rust #javascript #coding #programming #developer #webdev #rustlang #patternmatching #cleancode #codingtips

slides.title: Which branching is cleaner?
slides.problem: Handle multiple cases with complex data

a.label: JS switch

b.label: Rust match

slides.title: B wins. It's an expression.

verdicts.title: Statements, not expressions
verdicts.text: switch can't return a value directly. You need break. You can fall through accidentally. No exhaustive checking.

verdicts.title: Pattern matching + destructuring
verdicts.text: match returns a value. Destructures in place. The compiler forces you to handle every case. Zero fallthrough bugs.

slides.takeaway: Rust match is what JavaScript switch should have been.

slides.title: match does way more.

items.text: Returns a value — it's an expression

slides.teaser: Next: Rust ownership — explained for JS developers
